title: Akaki Tsereteli State University, Georgia – Atlas Mentor
description: Atlas Mentor guides MBBS aspirants applying to Akaki Tsereteli State University, Georgia — personalized support from application through enrollment.
body:
Akaki Tsereteli State University, Georgia

Atlas Mentor specializes in guiding students through their MBBS study abroad journey, particularly at Akaki Tsereteli State University, Georgia ensuring personalized and comprehensive support throughout the application and enrollment process.

  • By Altas Mentor
  • University In Georgia
  • July 15, 2024

  • Akaki Tsereteli State University, Georgia
# Georgia

Akaki Tsereteli State University, Georgia

Akaki Tsereteli State University (ATSU) was established in 1930 in Tbilisi, Georgia, and is now located in Kutaisi. It is a recognized and highly respected medical university, known for its quality education and experienced faculty. The university is particularly popular among students pursuing MBBS degrees and is ranked as one of the top medical schools in Georgia.

ATSU provides an excellent student-teacher ratio, ensuring personalized attention and support. The faculty consists of experienced doctorates and scientists who help students in their academic and professional development. The university also offers numerous extracurricular activities to help students discover and develop their talents and skills.

The campus features urban facilities, including well-maintained hostels with comfortable accommodations. The education cost at ATSU is affordable, making it a popular choice among Indian students. The university is recognized by the World Health Organization (WHO) and the National Medical Commission (NMC), which adds to its global reputation and allows graduates to practice medicine internationally.

Overall, Akaki Tsereteli State University offers a comprehensive and supportive environment for students aiming for a successful career in medicine.

Affiliation and Recognition of Akaki Tsereteli State University, Georgia

Akaki Tsereteli State University is widely recognized and affiliated with several prestigious medical councils and organizations, ensuring high educational standards and global credibility for its graduates.

  • World Health Organization (WHO): Recognized by WHO for maintaining global health standards.
  • National Medical Commission (NMC): Approved by NMC, allowing Indian graduates to practice in India.
  • World Federation Of Medical Education (WFME): Accredited by WFME for ensuring high-quality medical education.
  • Education Commission For Foreign Medical Graduates (ECFMG): Listed by ECFMG, enabling graduates to pursue further studies in the USA.
  • Foundation For Advancement Of International Medical Education And Research (FAIMER): Recognized by FAIMER for its contributions to international medical education.

Ranking of Akaki Tsereteli State University, Georgia

Akaki Tsereteli State University holds a significant position both nationally and internationally, reflecting its excellence in medical education and research. Here are the key points regarding its ranking:

  • Country Ranking: Ranked as the 5th best medical university in Georgia.
  • World Ranking: Holds the 5,901st position globally according to EduRank.

Faculties at Akaki Tsereteli State University, Georgia

Akaki Tsereteli State University offers a diverse range of faculties, each dedicated to providing high-quality education and fostering academic excellence in their respective fields:

  1. Faculty of Humanitarian: Focuses on humanities and social sciences, promoting critical thinking and cultural awareness.
  2. Faculty of Agriculture: Provides comprehensive education in agricultural sciences, supporting sustainable farming practices.
  3. Faculty of Medicine: Offers rigorous medical training, preparing students for careers in healthcare and medical research.
  4. Faculty of Exact and Natural Sciences: Specializes in scientific disciplines, encouraging innovation and scientific discovery.
  5. Faculty of Pedagogics: Trains future educators, emphasizing effective teaching methods and educational development.

Why Study MBBS at Akaki Tsereteli State University?

Choosing to study MBBS at Akaki Tsereteli State University offers numerous advantages that make it an excellent option for aspiring medical professionals:

  1. NMC-Approved University: The university is approved by the National Medical Commission (NMC), ensuring that the degree is recognized in India, allowing graduates to practice medicine there.

  2. Globally Recognized Courses: Courses are recognized by several prestigious organizations, including the European Council, UNESCO, and the World Health Organization (WHO). This global recognition enhances the university’s credibility and provides graduates with a strong international standing.

  3. English-Medium Education: For foreign students, the university offers medical courses in English, making it accessible to students from various countries and eliminating language barriers.

  4. Strong Emphasis on Practical Teaching: The curriculum is designed to prioritize practical teaching methods, ensuring that students gain hands-on experience and develop essential clinical skills.

  5. Enhanced Job Prospects: Graduating from a globally recognized institution opens up a wide range of job opportunities in the medical field, both in Georgia and internationally.

  6. Potential for Residency and Permanent Settlement in Europe: Completing your medical education at Akaki Tsereteli State University can provide pathways to residency and permanent settlement in Europe, offering a stable and prosperous future.

Eligibility Criteria for Studying MBBS at Akaki Tsereteli State University

Georgia is a popular destination for international students pursuing a medical degree. To study MBBS at Akaki Tsereteli State University (ATSU), you must meet the following eligibility criteria:

  1. Age:

    • You must be at least 17 years old on or before 31st December of the admission year.
    • There is no upper age limit.

  2. Qualification: Completion of Class 12th in Science with Physics, Chemistry, Biology (PCB), and English from a board recognized by Indian authorities.

  3. Minimum Qualifying Percentage:

    • General category (UR): 50% in 10+2.
    • Reserved categories (SC/OBC/ST): 45% in 10+2.

  4. Qualifying Entrance: NEET (National Eligibility cum Entrance Test) is required for Indian students.

  5. IELTS/TOEFL: Not required for admission

Documents Required for MBBS at Akaki Tsereteli State University, Georgia

When applying to Akaki Tsereteli State University for MBBS in Georgia, you will need to submit the following documents:

  1. 12th Standard Passing Certificate: Proof of completing Class 12 with Physics, Chemistry, Biology (PCB), and English.

  2. NEET UG Examination Qualified Scorecard: Scorecard from the NEET (National Eligibility cum Entrance Test) for Indian students.

  3. Transfer Certificate: Document certifying your transfer from the previous educational institution (if applicable).

  4. Migration Certificate: Certificate verifying your migration from the previous educational board (if applicable).

  5. Character Certificate: Certificate confirming your good conduct and behavior from the last attended institution.

  6. Passport: Valid passport for identification and visa purposes.

  7. 12 Passport Size Photographs: Recent passport-size photographs for various administrative purposes.

Duration of MBBS at Akaki Tsereteli State University

The MBBS course at Akaki Tsereteli State University spans six years, including:

  • Five years dedicated to theoretical medical education.
  • One year allocated for clinical internship.

Intake for Akaki Tsereteli State University

Akaki Tsereteli State University typically begins its MBBS course intake in September. As a highly esteemed institution in Georgia with limited seats for international students, the admission process starts in May. Prospective students are advised to apply early to secure their place.

Medium of Teaching at Akaki Tsereteli State University

The medium of instruction for the MBBS course at Akaki Tsereteli State University is English. Additionally, professors also teach Georgian to ensure students can effectively communicate with local patients during their internship period.

Akaki Tsereteli State University Facilities

Akaki Tsereteli State University, established 87 years ago, has an inclusive admission policy based on entrance examinations, welcoming international applicants. The university provides a range of academic and non-academic resources and services to students, including:

  • Library: Access to extensive academic resources.
  • Housing: Well-equipped hostel facilities.
  • Sports Facilities: Opportunities for physical activities.
  • Study Abroad and Exchange Programs: Enhancing global learning experiences.
  • Online Courses and Distance Learning: Flexible learning options.
  • Administrative Services: Support for student needs.
